text:
User: no, build it as an actual ios app -- I have xcode and everything! [...] Assistant: On it — real native SwiftUI app this time, full-bleed (no web-style cards), circuit/superset-aware, and a TikTok-style one-set-at-a-time flow. First let me check your toolchain so I target the right SDK (real Liquid Glass needs the Xcode 26 SDK).

Assistant: Perfect toolchain: **Xcode 26.6 / iOS 26.5 SDK** — real Liquid Glass APIs are available, and `xcodegen` is installed. Understood: a **mock** (mock data, no real backend) but a genuine native iOS app. I'll scaffold the project deterministically and have a Sonnet agent write the SwiftUI. [...] Targets iOS 26 Liquid Glass APIs throughout (`.glassEffect()`, `.glassEffect(.regular.tint(...).interactive(), in:)`, `GlassEffectContainer`, `.buttonStyle(.glass)` / `.glassProminent`), reserved strictly for floating controls per the hard design rules — no card containers anywhere. Pure SwiftUI, no third-party packages, Swift language mode 5 compatible, single `@main`. I did not run xcodebuild/xcodegen per your instructions.
